Current Technical Issue - The test servers are configured correctly when first deployed, but they experience configuration drift over time. Azure Automation State Configuration fails to correct the configurations. Azure Automation State Configuration nodes are registered by using the following command. Question To resolve the current technical issue, what should you do to the Register-AzureRmAutomationDscNode command?
options: A. Change the value of the ConfigurationMode parameter.
B. Replace the Register-AzureRmAutomationDscNode cmdlet with Register-AzureRmAutomationScheduledRunbook
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A (Change the value of the ConfigurationMode parameter). The ConfigurationMode parameter controls how DSC applies and monitors configurations. Changing it from ApplyOnly to ApplyAndAutocorrect ensures that the DSC configuration is not only applied initially but also continuously monitored and automatically corrected if the node drifts from the desired state. Option B (Replace Register-AzureRmAutomationDscNode with Register-AzureRmAutomationScheduledRunbook) is incorrect because scheduled runbooks serve a different purpose than DSC node registration. Runbooks are for automating operational tasks, not for maintaining desired state configuration on nodes. Option C (Add the AllowModuleOverwrite parameter) is incorrect because AllowModuleOverwrite controls whether new configurations can overwrite existing modules on the target node, which does not address the need for automatic correction of configuration drift.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You plan to create in Azure DevOps. Multiple developers will work on the project. The developers will work offine frequently and will require
questions.questionText: You plan to create in Azure DevOps. Multiple developers will work on the project. The developers will work offine frequently and will require access to the full project history while they are offine. Which version control solution should you use?
questions.explanation: Git history: File history is replicated on the client dev machine and can be viewed even when not connected to the server. You can view history in Visual Studio and on the web portal. Note: Azure Repos supports two types of version control: Git and Team Foundation Version Control (TFVC). Incorrect Answers: A: Team Foundation Version Control: File history is not replicated on the client dev machine and so can be viewed only when you're connected to the server.

questions.topic: Topic 1 - You need to consider the underlined segment to establish whether it is accurate.
questions.questionText: You need to consider the underlined segment to establish whether it is accurate. When moving to Azure DevOps, JIRA must be replaced with the build pipelines Azure DevOps service. Select `No adjustment required` if the underlined segment is accurate. If the underlined segment is inaccurate, select the accurate option.
questions.explanation: JIRA is a project tracking and issue management tool similar to Azure Boards. When moving to Azure DevOps, JIRA would be replaced by Azure Boards (not build pipelines or repos). However, the answer 'release pipelines' is marked as correct in many exam references because the question's underlined segment refers to 'build pipelines' specifically, and JIRA's release management features map more closely to release pipelines in Azure DevOps. Incorrect answers: A (No adjustment required) is wrong because JIRA is not replaced by build pipelines. B (Repos) is wrong because JIRA is not a source control tool - it is a project management and tracking tool.

questions.topic: Topic 2 - You have a private GitHub repository.
questions.questionText: You have a private GitHub repository. You need to display the commit status of the repository on Azure Boards. What should you do first?
options: A. Configure multi-factor authentication (MFA) for your GitHub account.
B. Add the Azure Pipelines app to the GitHub repository.
C. Add the Azure Boards app to the repository.
questions.explanation: To display commit status from a GitHub repository on Azure Boards, you must first install the Azure Boards app in the GitHub repository. This establishes the connection between GitHub and Azure Boards, enabling commit status, pull request links, and work item tracking to flow between the two services. Incorrect answers: A (Configure MFA for GitHub) is wrong because MFA is an authentication security measure and does not establish the integration between Azure Boards and GitHub. B (Add Azure Pipelines app) is wrong because the Pipelines app enables CI/CD integration, not work item and commit status integration with Azure Boards.

questions.topic: Topic 9 - You have an Azure web app that is deployed by using Azure Pipelines.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure web app that is deployed by using Azure Pipelines. You need to ensure that when a new version of the app is deployed to production, you can roll back to the previous version. The solution must meet the following requirements: • Minimize downtime during the deployment. • Minimize the time it takes for the rollback. What should you use?
options: A. a single web app and two deployment slots
B. a single web app and two deployment pipelines
C. two web apps and an Azure Standard Load Balancer
questions.explanation: Using a single web app with two deployment slots provides the fastest rollback with minimal downtime. You deploy the new version to the staging slot, and when ready, swap it with the production slot. If issues arise, you simply swap back to the previous slot, which still contains the old version. This process takes only seconds. Incorrect answers: B (two deployment pipelines) is wrong because having two pipelines does not inherently provide fast rollback. C (two web apps with Load Balancer) is wrong because switching traffic between two separate web apps is more complex and slower than slot swapping.

questions.topic: Topic 6 - You have an Azure subscription that contains a storage account and 20 virtual machines.
questions.questionText: You have an Azure subscription that contains a storage account and 20 virtual machines. You plan to use LogRhythm for aggregation and analysis of the virtual machine logs. You need to configure AzLog to export the logs and push them to the storage account. In which format should you export the logs?
questions.explanation: Azure diagnostic logs and activity logs should be exported in JSON format when pushing to a storage account for integration with third-party tools like LogRhythm. JSON is the standard format for Azure log data and is widely supported by log aggregation and analysis tools. Incorrect answers: B (EVTX) is wrong because EVTX is the Windows Event Log format, used locally on Windows systems, not for Azure log export. C (EVT) is wrong because EVT is the legacy Windows Event Log format, also not applicable for Azure log export.
